name: Ideal gas equation of state (PV = nRT)
conventions: T in kelvin (absolute temperature, not deg C or deg F)
P in pascal, V in m^3, n in mol when using R = 8.314 J/(mol K)
n is the amount of substance, not particle count
R is the universal gas constant — same for every gas
expectedLimits: low P, high T: exact (point particles + no interactions)
isothermal (T const): P V = const (Boyle's law)
high P or low T: van der Waals corrections required
T -> 0: ideal-gas law breaks (real gases liquefy / quantum statistics dominate)
